name: Switzerland
overview: Switzerland's revised Federal Act on Data Protection (revFADP) took effect on 1 September 2023, modernising the 1992 law to GDPR-comparable standards — privacy by design, processing records, breach notification, DPIAs — while keeping distinctive features such as criminal (not administrative) fines on responsible individuals. Switzerland holds EU adequacy and operates its own Swiss-US Data Privacy Framework since September 2024.

regulator.name: Federal Data Protection and Information Commissioner (FDPIC)

legal_instruments.title: Federal Act on Data Protection (FADP, revised)
legal_instruments.summary: Comprehensive law aligned with GDPR concepts: transparency duties, records of processing, DPIAs, breach notification to the FDPIC 'as soon as possible', genetic/biometric data as sensitive, and criminal fines up to CHF 250,000 on private individuals responsible for violations.

legal_instruments.title: Ordinance on Data Protection (DPO)
legal_instruments.summary: Implementing ordinance: minimum security requirements, modalities of information and access rights, breach-report content, and the annexed list of countries with adequate protection.

developments.title: Swiss-US Data Privacy Framework operational
developments.summary: The Federal Council recognised the Swiss-US DPF, allowing transfers to certified US companies without additional safeguards from 15 September 2024.

developments.title: EU renews Swiss adequacy
developments.summary: The European Commission's review of pre-GDPR adequacy decisions confirmed Switzerland continues to ensure adequate protection following the revFADP.

penalties: Criminal fines up to CHF 250,000 imposed on responsible private individuals (not companies) for intentional violations of information, disclosure or minimum-security duties; company may be fined up to CHF 50,000 where attributing conduct is disproportionate.
cross_border: Transfers permitted to countries on the Federal Council's adequacy list; otherwise safeguards (SCCs adapted to Swiss law, BCRs) or derogations. Swiss-US DPF covers certified US recipients.
